economic_notes: First Nation has 10% investment in Amik-BBF HydroKap LP and Amik-CTR HydroKap LP, 33% investment in 2454357 Ontario Inc. and 33% investment in Three Nations Drilling LP through 2454357 Ontario Inc. COFN TLE trust distributions of $1,072,504 received. Vehicle lease commitment at $14,323 annually ending October 2025.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED because the First Nation has not consolidated the Chapleau Ojibwe First Nation Treaty Land Entitlement Trust (created 2017), which constitutes a departure from Canadian public sector accounting standards. Effects of non-consolidation have not been determined. The COFN TLE Trust distributions of $1,072,504 are included in revenue but the Trust itself is not consolidated. Province of Ontario revenue of $62,600 and Wabun Tribal Council of $353,611 are included in total government transfers calculation. Resources revenue of $2,736,154 appears to be own-source revenue from resource operations. Prior year figures are restated per Note 14 (note not visible in provided pages). Demand loans of $122,945 are for vehicles. Long term debt is an RBC mortgage at prime + 2.11%.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
